description: This is a satellite image, specifically a SPOT-5 (Satellite Pour l'Observation de la Terre) image with 2.5 m resolution, captured on 2011-08-11, showing a transformed salt marsh in Doñana National Park, Spain. The image uses a 1/2/3 Band Combination, typically representing false-colour infrared where active vegetation appears red. The right side of the image depicts the dark blue coastal water. Inland, a significant area of reddish vegetation is visible, containing several elongated, lighter-coloured features that suggest former agricultural plots or drainage channels, indicating a transformed marsh without current agricultural use. Dark, meandering lines represent water bodies or rivers, such as the Guadalquivir river estuary mentioned in the surrounding context for similar images. The image source is CNES 2011©, distributed by Airbus DS/Spot Image.
